trajectory.description: Observation: MetricGAN+ baseline PESQ=3.134, custom ConvTasNet PESQ=2.292. The time-domain ConvTasNet significantly underperforms. Analysis: Frequency-domain models with multi-resolution STFT loss tend to work much better for SE. The current model also uses only L1 loss which is suboptimal. Decision: Build a frequency-domain CRN with encoder-decoder, LSTM bottleneck, skip connections, and multi-resolution STFT loss. This architecture is proven for SE and should be more competitive with MetricGAN+.

trajectory.description: Observation: FreqCRN training failed because MultiResolutionSTFTLoss expects 3D input [B,C,L] but receives 2D [B,L]. Also, tanh activation bounds complex ratio mask to [-1,1], limiting speech amplification. Analysis: Need to fix MRSTFT loss input shape by adding channel dimension, and remove tanh to allow unbounded complex ratio mask. Decision: Fix both issues, add gradient clipping for stability with unbounded mask, and train for 50 epochs.

Observation: MRSTFT loss crashes because window size (600) doesn't match win_length parameter (512) in stft call. The train.py was also truncated so I need to write a complete version. Analysis: Fix by making win_length=n_fft for each resolution. Also need to ensure the full train.py is correct including the training loop, evaluation, and checkpoint saving. Decision: Write complete fixed train.py and model.py, train for 50 epochs with proper MRSTFT + SI-SNR + L1 loss combination.

trajectory.description: Observation: FreqCRN val PESQ 2.34 (bad), MetricGAN+ baseline 3.134 (good), eval failed due to torch.load weights_only issue. Analysis: The unbounded complex ratio mask is likely causing instability. Magnitude masking with bounded output and simpler loss should work much better. Decision: Fix predict.py with weights_only=False, save MetricGAN+ checkpoint as safety net, write new magnitude-masking CRN model, and prepare for training.

Observation: Best PESQ is 3.134 (MetricGAN+ baseline). FreqCRN got only 2.34. Need to beat 3.134. Analysis: Complex spectral mapping (predicting real+imaginary residuals) can improve both magnitude and phase, unlike magnitude masking which preserves noisy phase. Zero-initialized residual learning ensures stability. Decision: Write a clean ComplexMappingCRN model with residual learning, train for 50 epochs, then evaluate. If it gets close to 3.134, can try ensemble with MetricGAN+.

CRN has better cbak (3.31 vs 2.90) and stoi (0.94 vs 0.93), suggesting it handles noise better but speech quality worse. An ensemble combining both models' strengths might improve PESQ. Decision: Test ensemble of MetricGAN+ and CRN on val data with different weights, then also try post-processing approaches. Pick the best and submit eval.
